text: The surgeon identifies purulent peritonitis and a perforation in the sigmoid colon. They begin medial to lateral mobilization of the descending colon, sectioning the inferior mesenteric vein and detaching the mesocolon from the deep plane. The splenic flexure remains partially mobilized due to diverticulitis. After fully mobilizing the ascending colon, the left ureter is identified. The mesosigmoid is mobilized while preserving the artery's vascularization. The colon is sectioned with a stapler at the promontory level, and the mesocolon is sectioned near the colon.
The surgeon performs a mechanical circular anastomosis, detaching reinforcement points. No protective ileostomy or ghost ileostomy is created during the procedure.
